msItem.title: De diebus infaustis
msItem.note: Ἐναντίαι ἡμέραι τῶν μηνῶν περὶ ἀρρώστων.
Inc. αʹ· ἂν πέσει εἰς ἀσθένειαν. Des. λαʹ· κινδυνεύει βʹ καὶ ὑγιαίνει.
Followed by a table calculating the number of days (50) between 23 January and 13 March, probably intended for determining either the onset of an illness or the date of recovery.

msItem.author: Hippocrates
msItem.title: Aphorisms
msItem.note: Ends with the seventh aphorism; followed by a later closing title.

msItem.author: Ps.-Hippocrates
msItem.title: Letter to Ptolemy
msItem.incipit: Ἱπποκράτους ἐστὶ δὲ ὁ λόγος οὗτος, πρῶτον μὲν περὶ τῆς ἐγκυκλίου σφαίρας...
msItem.explicit: καὶ καμάτους καὶ καὶ ἀφροδισιεύειν (?). Τέλος ὧδε πέφυκε τοῦ Ἱπποκράτους λόγος, ἔτι περὶ μενῶν καὶ ὀνήσιμος ἄγαν. Τέλος.
msItem.note: Excerpts cf. J. F. Boissonade, Anecdota Graeca III (Paris, 1831), 423.1–425.1.

msItem.author: Galen
msItem.title: De curandi ratione per venae sectionem
msItem.note: Three extracts, considerably reworked; cf. Kühn XI, 267.1ine 8–316.line 7.
msItem.explicit: ἔχοντοϲ τοῦ περὶ τὸ κέντρον μορίου παντόϲ.

msItem.author: Galen
msItem.title: De anatomicis administrationibus
msItem.note: Extract; cf. Kühn II, 537.1ine 2–538. line 2.
msItem.explicit: τὸ σῶμα ἐστὶν οἰκεῖον ἐν ἀπασιν.

msItem.author: Stephanus Magnes
msItem.title: Empirica
msItem.incipit: Ἄβροτα διατηρεῖ σκόλιχον καὶ μυὸν καὶ τρωγλοδύτην
msItem.note: Attributed in the manuscript to Paul of Aegina.

msItem.author: Ps.-Paul of Aegina
msItem.note: Τοῦ δὲ Παύλου ἔλειμμα πρὸς ῥαγάδος χειρῶν καὶ ποδῶν, πρὸς τε τὰς χιωνέδας ἐν αὐτοῖς ἐγκαύσεις
msItem.title: Recipes for ointments

msItem.author: Ps.-Luke the Evangelist
msItem.title: Alation
msItem.explicit: ὀδόντας καλουμένου στηρίζει, καὶ εὐάζεται δὲ οὕτως.
msItem.note: Ed. J. L. Ideler, Physici et medici Graeci minores I (Berlin, 1841), 297. lines 1–9.

msItem.author: Paul of Aegina
msItem.title: Epitome medica
msItem.note: Extracts from book 5, chapters 1-17; 20; 18; 21; 19; 22-31; 36-48.
For the compilation, see Gabrielle Lherminier, Édition critique et traduction annotée du livre V des Epitomae Medicae de Paul d’Égine, Thèse de doctorat, EPHE, Paris, 2013, pp. 347-349.

msItem.author: Simeon Seth
msItem.title: Medical and dietary treatises
msItem.note: Συμεὼν μαγίστρου τοῦ Σὴθ τοῦ Ἀντιοχέως, Περὶ ὑγιεινῆς πραγματείας διὰ τῆς τῶν ἕξ αἰτίων συμμετρίας.

Includes 1: (ff. 29r-v) Pinax de alimentorum facultatibus 2: (ff. 29v-30v) De vita sana edited in M. Cronier, A. Guardasole, and A. Pietrobelli, ‘Syméon Seth, Sur le manuel de santé d’après l’équilibre des six causes’, in M. Cronier, A. Guardasole, and A. Pietrobelli (eds.), De Bagdad à Constantinople : le transfert des savoirs médicaux (XIe–XIVe s.) (Leuven–Paris–Bristol, CT, 2023) 35–56. 3: (ff. 30v-31v) Adversus Galenum edited in Marie Cronier, Alessia Guardasole, Caroline Magdelaine and Antoine Pietrobelli, Galien en procès à Byzance : l’Antirrhétique de Syméon Seth, Galenos 9 (2015), pp. 71–121: 108-121. and 4: (ff. 31v-49r) De alimentorum facultatibus as edited by Bernhard Langkavel Simeonis Sethi Syntagma de alimentorum facultatibus (Leipzig, 1868).

msItem.title: Antidotes
msItem.note: Ἐξ ἑτέρων τινῶν εἴτε ἀπὸ πεῖραν εἴτε διὰ διηγήσεων εἴτε καὶ μὴ
msItem.incipit: Περὶ τῶν ὠφελούντων εἴς τε τὰς τῶν δηλητηρίων πόσεις
msItem.explicit: Τὸ ἦπαρ τῶν χοίρων σὺν οἴνῳ λαμβανόμενον πρὸς τὰ ἰοβόλα δήγματα ὠφελεῖ

msItem.title: Substances useful and harmful
msItem.note: Ὅσα ὠφέλιμα καὶ βλαπτικὰ κεφαλῇ
msItem.explicit: αἱ ἀρχὲς τῶν χηνῶν εὐεργετοῦσι προσηκόντως

msItem.author: Julius Africanus
msItem.title: Cestes
msItem.note: Ἐκ τῶν Ἀφρικανοῦ Κεστῶν ὅπερ ἐστὶν αὐτοῦ βιβλίον ιγʹ, κεφάλαιον τοῦ αὐτοῦ βιβλίου μβʹ, καθαρτικὰ ἁπλᾶ
msItem.explicit: ὀρῷ τῷ ἐκ γάλακτος μιχθεὶς καὶ ποθεὶς.
msItem.note: Edited M. Wallraff, C. Scardino, L. Mecella, C. Guignard, and W. Adler, Iulius Africanus. Cesti: the Extant Fragments (Griechischen christlichen Schriftsteller der ersten drei Jahrhunderte, Neue Folge 18; Berlin–Boston, 2012), pp. 188-189..

msItem.author: Paul of Aegina
msItem.title: Epitome medica
msItem.note: Extract from book. I, chapter 100 known as “Letter of Diocles to Antigonus”
Ἐκ τῆς Παύλου τοῦ Αἰγινήτου βίβλου τὸ ἔσχατον κεφάλαιον τοῦ πρώτου λόγου, τὸ ἑκατὸν κεφάλαιον, τὸ ἔχον ἐπιγραφὴν οὕτως· «Διοκλῆς Ἀντιγόνῳ».
msItem.explicit: εἰς δὲ ἕως τροπῶν χειμερινῶν ἡμέρα μεʹ.

msItem.author: Galen
msItem.title: De alimentorum facultatibus
msItem.note: Ἐκ τοῦ γʹ λόγου τοῦ περὶ τροφῶν.
1. (ff. 51v-54v) Book 3, pinax and extracts Inc. Αἱ σάρκες τῶν ζώων αἵματος γεννῶται. Des. εἰς δὲ θήρων χειμῶνας τῶν λευκῶν.
2. (ff. 54r-55r) Book 2, untitled extracts. Inc. Περὶ ἐλαίων, ὀξύρραφα. Des. καὶ παχὺς ἔχουσι χυμούς συμφέρει.
3. (f. 55r-55v) Extracts from Dioscorides, Paul of Aigina and Galen

msItem.author: Galen
msItem.title: Recipes for depilation
msItem.note: Ἀφανιστικὰ τριχῶν.
Contains two recipes. 1. Inc. Ἀφανίζειν τὰς τρίχας εἰς τὸ παντελές. Des. καὶ κατὰ βραχὺ φθείρονται. 2. Inc. Λαβὼν κύνα καὶ ἀποκτείνας. Des. καὶ ἀφανισθήσονται αἱ τρίχες.

support.p: Paper throughout, with the same stock used for the original front flyleaves and the text block; fine vertical chain-lines, folded in folio format. Watermarks: tower (cf. Briquet 15908, c. 1449), leaf/ (cf. Piccard XII, 86–88, c. 1447), and scissors (f. 54 only).

collation.p: viii (ff. 1–8), 4 × i (ff. 9–12), 8 × viii (ff. 13–76), vi (ff. 77–82), 15 × viii (ff. 83–202). Quire signatures, mostly scribal, occur in the centre of the upper margin of each quire’s first page and, except for ff. 13–82, in the centre of the lower margin of each quire’s last page. ff. 57–68 were probably formed from two ternions.

layoutDesc.layout: Two columns, 39 lines; written area c. 210 × 65 mm per column; intercolumn space c. 15 mm.

handDesc.handNote: Principal scribe: probably Georgios Kalophrenas (ff. iir col. 1, l. 1–col. 2, l. 19; ff. 1r–55v).
Secondary scribe, unidentified (ff. iir col. 2, l. 20–iiir); ff. 56–68 are blank but were intended from the outset to be written upon.

decoDesc.decoNote: Very sparse rubrication in red ink: a decorative band on f. 1r and major initials, often omitted; rubrication unfinished.

binding.p: Light-brown morocco binding of the nineteenth century, with the title HIPPOCRATES gilt on the fore-edge; lower board detached.

history.provenance: Copied in the workshop of Michael Apostolis, probably from a Constantinopolitan exemplar; the workshop was later continued by his son Aristoboulos Apostolis (Arsenius of Monemvasia).

Acquired soon after copying by Giovanni Scandoler, Venetian physician resident in Crete; on f. viiiv he added a note on his doctorate in medicine, taken at the Collegio Medico of Venice on 1 Aug. 1471.
Later in Greek hands; notes by a Cretan of the late fifteenth or early sixteenth century, and a French pencil note on f. 22r probably by Charles Daremberg.
Later owned by Francesco Barozzi and Jacopo Barozzi; entered the Bodleian Library in 1629.

recordHist.source: Updated from Marie Cronier, Alessia Guardasole, Caroline Magdelaine and Antoine Pietrobelli, Galien en procès à Byzance : l’Antirrhétique de Syméon Seth, Galenos 9 (2015), pp. 71–121: 101-106. Summary description abbreviated from the Quarto Catalogue (H. O. Coxe, Greek Manuscripts, Quarto Catalogues I, repr. 1969, with corrections, from the ed. of 1853). Summary Catalogue, Vol. 2 Part 1, p. 9 Quarto Catalogue, Col. 390 Quarto Catalogue, Cols. 391–392

listBibl.bibl: Marie Cronier, Alessia Guardasole, Caroline Magdelaine and Antoine Pietrobelli, Galien en procès à Byzance : l’Antirrhétique de Syméon Seth, Galenos 9 (2015),71–121.

M. Wallraff, C. Scardino, L. Mecella, C. Guignard, and W. Adler, Iulius Africanus. Cesti: the Extant Fragments (Griechischen christlichen Schriftsteller der ersten drei Jahrhunderte, Neue Folge 18; Berlin–Boston, 2012), p. lxxvii.

M. Cronier, A. Guardasole, and A. Pietrobelli, Syméon Seth, Sur le manuel de santé d’après l’équilibre des six causes: Introduction, editio princeps, traduction annotée, in M. Cronier, A. Guardasole, and A. Pietrobelli (eds.), De Bagdad à Constantinople : le transfert des savoirs médicaux (XIe–XIVe s.). Actes du colloque international de Reims, 24–25 mai 2018 (Orient et Méditerranée 42; Leuven–Paris–Bristol, CT, 2023) 35–56

ref.title: Pinakes | Πίνακες: Textes et manuscrits grecs

revisionDesc.change: James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/jamescummings/Bodleian-msDesc-ODD/blob/master/convertTolkien2Bodley.xsl
